docs.title: Bulk Create Item-Driven Cycle Count Tasks
Overview
docs.text: Creates item-driven cycle count tasks in bulk across specified or all locations. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text: POST /wms/lgfapi/v10/entity/location/cc_item_task/bulk_create

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Basic Authentication (username + password)

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: ```json { "parameters": { "item_id_list": [227989,227990], "facility_id": 648, "company_id": 369 }, "options": { "location_id_list": [132873], "priority": 20, "description": "CC-LOCN-BY-ITEM" } }

docs.title: Flow
docs.text: Author → GitHub → CI/CD → Build → Deploy → Live Docs

docs.title: Explanation
docs.text:
1. Content is written in Markdown
  2. Pushed to GitHub repository
  3. GitHub Actions triggers build
  4. MkDocs generates static site
  5. Deployed to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
In warehouse operations, a wide range of SKUs and suppliers results in barcodes of various formats, structures, and qualifiers. Not all scanned barcodes arrive in a format that matches your Warehouse Management System (WMS) requirements. These variations can lead to inconsistencies during scanning and downstream processing.

The Barcode Preprocessing (BarcodePreProcView) UI allows you to configure rules for preprocessing scanned barcodes. You can define barcode matching conditions and specify logic used to transform barcode values. When a scanned barcode meets the defined criteria, the system applies the configured preprocessing rules and updates the barcode before further processing.

Note:

  • Rules can be configured only in Redwood UI format.
  • Rules are user group-specific.

docs.title: Processing Flow
docs.text: When a barcode is scanned, the system processes it in the following order: 1. Match the scanned barcode against configured preprocessing rules (by sequence). 2. Apply the corresponding update logic (if matched). 3. Stop further rule evaluation. 4. Pass the updated barcode for downstream processing.

docs.title: Key Capabilities
docs.text: Use barcode preprocessing rules to: - Standardize barcode formats across suppliers. - Extract or restructure barcode segments from the scanned barcode. - Validate barcode input in real time. - Reject invalid barcodes before they disrupt operations, reducing manual intervention and errors. - Adapt to new barcode formats without code changes.

docs.title: Match Functions
docs.text: Function Description Min Length Matches based on minimum length Exact Length Matches exact barcode length Begins With Matches prefix Contains Matches substring Regex Match Matches regex pattern

docs.title: Update Functions
docs.text: Function Description Substring Extract part of barcode Strip String Remove specific text Strip Regex Remove using regex Insert At Insert string at position Regex Replace Advanced transformations

docs.title: Example
docs.text: Input Barcode: ARTSSKU1234567 Output: SKU123

docs.title: Key Behavior
docs.text:
• Rules execute in sequence
  • First match stops evaluation
  • Updates run sequentially :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

docs.title: Display Additional Item Property
docs.text: Enhancement to mobile transactions to display extra item details. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Faster item identification
  • Improved accuracy
  • Reduced reliance on item codes

docs.title: New Parameter
docs.text:
extra-item-property-disp

Controls which item property is displayed during transactions.

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Default: No additional property
  • Fallback: Uses description if missing :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}
